titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Georgia O'Keeffe Writings and Other Papers
titlestmt.author: by Michael L. Forstrom

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 515 digital files.

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

did.unittitle: Georgia O'Keeffe Writings and Other Papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the collection of Juan Hamilton. Purchased from Sotheby's sale, "Alfred Stieglitz, Georgia O'Keeffe, Juan Hamilton: Passage," March 2020 (lots 13, 45, 46, 53, 57, 62, 70, 74, 82, 84, 96, 100, 107) on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund and Donald Windham-Sandy Campbell Collection Endowment Fund, 2020.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Georgia O'Keeffe Writings and Other Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Georgia O'Keeffe Writings and Other Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Georgia O'Keeffe (1887-1986)
bioghist.p: Georgia O'Keeffe (1887-1986) was an American painter and artist.

The "History of the Archive" note in the finding aid for the Alfred Stieglitz / Georgia O'Keeffe Archive (YCAL MSS 85) documents the custodial history of the bulk of O'Keeffe's papers and the construction of the Stieglitz / O'Keeffe archive circa 1949-1992.

Following Georgia O'Keeffe's death in 1986, materials in the Georgia O'Keeffe Writings and Other Papers (YCAL MSS 1381) were in the custody of Juan Hamilton until acquired by the library in 2020. Juan Hamilton is an American sculptor and painter who worked as an assistant to Georgia O'Keeffe from 1973 to 1986, during which time he became the executor and principal beneficiary of her estate. Following O'Keeffe's death, much of what was was left to him was turned over to her family's estate and select materials remained in his possession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of writings by Georgia O'Keeffe and other papers. Writings by O'Keeffe include drafts and other materials relating to her autobiographical book, Georgia O'Keeffe (New York: Viking Press, 1976), drafts and a galley proof for her "Introduction" to Georgia O'Keeffe, a portrait (New York: Metropolitan Museum of Art, 1978), the catalog produced for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's exhibition of portraits of O'Keeffe by Stieglitz, and drafts for several shorter works. Other papers include an address book, one autograph letter from O'Keeffe to Juan Hamilton, a set of gelatin silver prints by Todd Webb of O'Keeffe paintings in a 1946 exhibition, material relating to Stieglitz, and a recipe card file.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Writings, 1970-1982. II. Other Papers, [1920?]-1970s.

corpname.part: Metropolitan Museum of Art (New York, N.Y.)

did.unittitle: Writings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Georgia O'Keeffe and Other Writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. contains writings by Georgia O'Keeffe. The first subseries consists of drafts and other materials relating to an autobiographical project, identified here as "Book II," that culminated with the publication, Georgia O'Keeffe (New York: Viking Press, 1976). There are autograph manuscript drafts by O'Keeffe as well as typescript drafts, some corrected, and in some cases, handwritten transcriptions. The transcriptions, typescript drafts, and corrections appear to be the work of Virginia F. Robinson. Robinson's contribution is broadly acknowledged in a short November 2, 1974 statement by O'Keeffe (Box 1, folder 1 and Box 2, folder 13). Both Robinson and Juan Hamilton are acknowledged in the published version of the work.

In addition to the drafts and other materials for the autobiographical Georgia O'Keeffe project (1976), there are drafts for other shorter works by O'Keeffe in a second subseries for Other Writings. Many of these writings, based on similar handwriting and foolscrap folio paper, appear to date from the same general 1970s period, with a few files dating from 1978 to 1982. While it is possible some of these pieces were conceived as part of the autobiographical project, or considered for the Viking Press publication, they came to the library foldered separately and in separate envelopes, and they are arranged alphabetically by title in the Other Writings subseries.

Other Writings includes a typescript draft of an essay, "Events leading to lawsuit," in which O'Keeffe touches on contributions by Robinson and Hamilton to the 1976 Viking Press book before discussing, at greater length, the circumstances surrounding her suit against long-time associate, dealer, and executor Doris Bry. There are also drafts and a galley proof for O'Keeffe's "Introduction" to Georgia O'Keeffe, a portrait (New York: Metropolitan Museum of Art, 1978), the catalog produced for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's exhibition of portraits of O'Keeffe by Alfred Stieglitz.
Some titles in Series I taken from typescript drafts, folders, or envelopes, likely provided by Robinson or Hamilton.

did.unittitle: Statement by O'Keeffe regarding drafts of Georgia O'Keeffe , typescript, signed by O'Keeffe and Virginia F. Robertson (photocopy)

Other Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by material type and heading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. Other Papers contains other, miscellaneous, material relating to O'Keeffe dating circa 1920 through the 1970s. Papers include an address book, an undated autograph letter from O'Keeffe to Juan Hamilton, material relating to Stieglitz, a set of 23 gelatin silver prints by Todd Webb of O'Keeffe paintings in a 1946 exhibition at The American Place, and a recipe card file. The recipe card file consists of over 200 recipes either written by hand, typed out, or on clippings.

scopecontent.p: Includes two photographs of unidentified man

scopecontent.p: Includes 23 gelatin silver prints by Todd Webb of artwork by O'Keeffe
